title: In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhead: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: Ending the war with Iran comes at the cost of major US concessions
French mural honoring 11-year-old Lyhanna's death faces uncertain future despite strong public support
Ukrainian drone assault on Moscow undermines Putin's promises of victory
subhead: The Russian capital suffered its largest aerial attack since 2022 on Thursday morning. Despite strict internet censorship, some Muscovites are voicing their fears and frustrations quietly, through messaging apps.

title: Mont-Saint-Michel will no longer be managed by France's National Monuments Center
subhead: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mont-Saint-Michel will now be managed exclusively by the public industrial and commercial establishment (EPIC). The iconic Norman landmark was in a months-long standoff between the two operators, EPIC and the National Monuments Center (CMN), which had been jointly managing the site.

title: Wealthy taxpayers clean out their holdings, keeping their homes, sports cars and gold bars safe
subhead: The new 20% tax on 'luxury' assets held in personal holdings is prompting owners to quickly remove them. As a result, the measure risks bringing in almost nothing.

title: 'Incidents multiply in the Baltic Sea, where the Russian navy 'now operates in a completely overt manner'
subhead: After a Russian frigate fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel on June 16, Rear Admiral Johan Norlén, chief of staff of the Swedish navy, describes an increasingly tense climate in Baltic waters.

subhead: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.

title: Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' aliens have nothing to do with those of Trump
subhead: Days before the film was released worldwide, the White House co-opted the imagery of 'aliens' to further its rhetoric and anti-immigration policy.

title: French environment minister's absence from public debate underscores government's climate inaction
subhead: Appiointed in October 2025, Monique Barbut is struggling to make her mark. This situation goes beyond her as an individual and highlights how the government has abandoned the issue.
